nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Benedict
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Church Latin benedictus ‘blessed’. This was the name of the saint ( c.480– c.550 ) who composed the Benedictine rule of Christian monastic life that is still followed in essence by all Western orders. He was born near Spoleto in Umbria, central Italy. After studying in Rome, he went to live as a hermit at Subiaco, and later organized groups of followers and imitators into monastic cells. In c.529 he moved to Monte Cassino, where he founded the great monastery that is still the centre of the Benedictine order. His rule is simple, restrained, and practical. See also Bennett .

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: Benedict de Blakenham I, of Chelmsworth, Lackford de Blakenham was born about 1172, in Great Blakenham, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. He had at least 1 son with Unknown. He died about 1231, in Great Blakenham, Suffolk, England, at the age of 61.

ja.lifeSummary: Benedict de Blakenham I, of Chelmsworth, Lackford de Blakenhamは約1172年にイギリスイギリススフォルクグレアト ブラケンハムで生まれた。彼はUnknownとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は約1231年にイギリススフォルクグレアト ブラケンハムで61歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Benedict de Blakenham I, of Chelmsworth, Lackford de Blakenham(은)는 대략 1172년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 그리트 블레이켄함에서 태어났다. 그는 Unknown(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 대략 1231년 영국 수폴크 그리트 블레이켄함에서 61세의 나이로 사망했다.
